devanagari: दिवोदासादिवंशकथनम् ऋक्षवंशे पाण्डवादि उत्पत्तिश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । मित्रेयुश्च दिवोदासात् च्यवनः तत्सुतो नृप । सुदासः सहदेवोऽथ सोमको जन्...
english_devnagari: divodāsādivaṃśakathanam ṛkṣavaṃśe pāṇḍavādi utpattiśca śrīśuka uvāca mitreyuśca divodāsāt cyavanaḥ tatsuto nṛpa sudāsaḥ sahadevo

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
From Divodāsa was born Mitreyu, whose sons were Cyavana, Sudāsa, Sahadeva and Somaka. Somaka had a hundred sons of whom Jantu was the eldest, and Pṛṣata, the youngest. His son was Drupada whose daughter was Draupadī, and sons were Dhṛṣṭadyumna and others.

devanagari: तस्य पुत्रशतं तेषां यवीयान् पृषतः सुतः । द्रुपदो द्रौपदी तस्यजज्ञे धृष्टद्युम्नादयः सुताः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya putraśataṃ teṣāṃ yavīyān pṛṣataḥ sutaḥ drupado draupadī tasyajajñe dhṛṣṭadyumnādayaḥ sutāḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
From Divodāsa was born Mitreyu, whose sons were Cyavana, Sudāsa, Sahadeva and Somaka. Somaka had a hundred sons of whom Jantu was the eldest, and Pṛṣata, the youngest. His son was Drupada whose daughter was Draupadī, and sons were Dhṛṣṭadyumna and others.

devanagari: धृष्टद्युम्नात् धृष्टकेतुः भार्म्याः पाञ्चालका इमे । योऽजमीढसुतो ह्यन्य ऋक्षः संवरणस्ततः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: dhṛṣṭadyumnāt dhṛṣṭaketuḥ bhārmyāḥ pāñcālakā ime yo
translation: From Dhṛṣṭadyumna appeared Dhṛṣṭaketu. These are the descendants of Bharmyāśva, also known as Pāñcālakas or Pāñcāla. Ajamīḍha had another son Ṛkṣa from whom was born Saṃvaraṇa.

devanagari: तपत्यां सूर्यकन्यायां कुरुक्षेत्रपतिः कुरुः । परीक्षित् सुधनुर्जह्नुः निषधाश्व कुरोः सुताः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tapatyāṃ sūryakanyāyāṃ kurukṣetrapatiḥ kuruḥ parīkṣit sudhanurjahnuḥ niṣadhāśva kuroḥ sutāḥ

translation: Saṃvaraṇa begot on Tapatī, the daughter of the Sun-god, Kuru, the Lord of the Kurukṣetra. Kuru had Parīkṣit (the first, an ancestor of the hearer of the Bh. P.) Sudhanu, Jahnu and Niṣadhāśva as his sons.

devanagari: सुहोत्रोऽभूत् सुधनुषः च्यवनोऽथ ततः कृती । वसुस्तस्योपरिचरो बृहद्रथमुखास्ततः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: suhotro vasustasyoparicaro bṛhadrathamukhāstataḥ
translation: From Sudhanu was born Suhotra whose son was Cyavana, his son was Kṛti, the father of Uparicara Vasu. From him were born Bṛhadratha and other sons.

devanagari: कुशाम्बमत्स्यप्रत्यग्र चेदिपाद्याश्च चेदिपाः । बृहद्रथात्कुशाग्रोऽभूत् ऋषभस्तस्य तत्सुतः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: kuśāmbamatsyapratyagra cedipādyāśca cedipāḥ bṛhadrathātkuśāgro
translation: (Out of them) Kuśāmba, Matsya, Pratyagra, Cedipa and others were the rulers of the Cedi country (Modern Bundelkhand and the region roundabout). From Bṛhadratha was born Kuśāgra whose son was Ṛṣabha.

devanagari: जज्ञे सत्यहितोऽपत्यं पुष्पवान् तत्सुतो जहुः । अन्यस्यामपि भार्यायां शकले द्वे बृहद्रथात् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: jajñe satyahito anyasyāmapi bhāryāyāṃ śakale dve bṛhadrathāt

translation: Ṛṣabha begot Satyahita, whose son was Puṣpavān. His son was Jahu. Bṛhadratha had two parts (of a child) born by another wife.

devanagari: ये मात्रा बहिरुत्सृष्टे जरया चाभिसन्धिते । जीव जीवेति क्रीडन्त्या जरासन्धोऽभवत् सुतः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: ye mātrā bahirutsṛṣṭe jarayā cābhisandhite jīva jīveti krīḍantyā jarāsandho

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [ablative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]

translation: Those parts were thrown away by the mother. A demoness Jarā, out of sport had joined them muttering the words ‘Live! Live. And lo! A son called Jarāsandha (one joined by Jarā) appeared.

devanagari: ततश्च सहदेवोऽभूत् सोमापिर्यत् श्रुतश्रवाः । परीक्षिद् अनपत्योऽभूत् सुरथो नाम जाह्नवः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tataśca sahadevo parīkṣid anapatyo

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Of him was born Sahadeva whose son was Somāpī, who begot Śrutaśravas. (Out of the sons of Kuru) Parīkṣit was childless. The son of Jahnu was Suratha.

devanagari: ततो विदूरथस्तस्मात् सार्वभौमस्ततोऽभवत् । जयसेनस्तत् तनयो राधिकोऽतोऽयुताय्वभूत् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tato vidūrathastasmāt sārvabhaumastato jayasenastat tanayo rādhiko
translation: Suratha’s son was Vidūratha, from whom was born Sārvabhauma. His son was Jayasena, whose son was Rādhika; from whom appeared Ayuta.

devanagari: ततश्च क्रोधनस्तस्मात् देवातिथिरमुष्य च । ऋष्यस्तस्य दिलीपोऽभूत् प्रतीपस्तस्य चात्मजः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tataśca krodhanastasmāt devātithiramuṣya ca ṛṣyastasya dilīpo
translation: From him sprang up Krodhana whose son was Devātithi. His son was Ṛṣya, from whom was born Dilīpa. He had a son Pratīpa.

devanagari: देवापिः शान्तनुस्तस्य बाह्लीक इति चात्मजाः । पितृराज्यं परित्यज्य देवापिस्तु वनं गतः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: devāpiḥ śāntanustasya bāhlīka iti cātmajāḥ pitṛrājyaṃ parityajya devāpistu vanaṃ gataḥ

translation: He (Pratīpa) had three sons—Devāpi, Śantanu and Bālhīka. Devāpi abdicated his ancestral throne and repaired to forest (for his spiritual advancement).

devanagari: अभवत् शन्तनू राजा प्राङ्महाभिषसंज्ञितः । यं यं कराभ्यां स्पृशति जीर्णं यौवनमेति सः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: abhavat śantanū rājā prāṅmahābhiṣasaṃjñitaḥ yaṃ yaṃ karābhyāṃ spṛśati jīrṇaṃ yauvanameti saḥ

translation: (After Devāpi’s departure to forest) Śantanu who was formerly designated as Mahābhiṣa (a great physician), came to the throne. Whomsoever he touched with his hand—even if he be old—got rejuvenated again. And the old man got immediate and complete relief (from all ailments). By virtue of this (miraculous) gift, he was called Śantanu. When the rain-god did not rain for twelve years in his kingdom,

devanagari: शान्तिमाप्नोति चैवाग्र्यां कर्मणा तेन शन्तनुः । समा द्वादश तद्राज्ये न ववर्ष यदा विभुः ॥ १४ ॥

Whomsoever he touched with his hand—even if he be old—got rejuvenated again. And the old man got immediate and complete relief (from all ailments). By virtue of this (miraculous) gift, he was called Śantanu. When the rain-god did not rain for twelve years in his kingdom,

[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rāj ], [accusative single from √ rāj ]

translation: Śantanu was advised by Brāhmaṇas that inasmuch as he, though a younger brother, enjoyed the kingship (superseding his elder brother) and had become a parivettā[1] (a usurper), and for the prosperity of the capital and the kingdom, he should immediately restore the kingdom to his elder brother, Devāpi (when alone it will rain).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes the following definition of Parivettā from a Smṛti text:
dārā'gnihotra-saṃyogaṃ kurute yo'graje sthite /
parivettā sa vijñeyaḥ, parivittis tu pūrvajaḥ //
He who accepts a wife or Agnihotra (fire-worship) before his elder brother does so, is called parivettā and the brother so superseded Parivitti.

devanagari: एवमुक्तो द्विजैर्ज्येष्ठं छन्दयामास सोऽब्रवीत् । तन्मंत्रिप्रहितैर्विप्रैः वेदाद् विभ्रंशितो गिरा ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: evamukto dvijairjyeṣṭhaṃ chandayāmāsa so tanmaṃtriprahitairvipraiḥ vedād vibhraṃśito girā

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: When thus addressed (advised) by Brāhmaṇas, he conciliated him (Devāpi) and persuaded him to accept the kingdom. Devāpi, however, was made to go astray from the path of Vedas by the words of Brāhmaṇas who were deputed by his ministers (especially by his minister (Aśmarāva).

devanagari: वेदवादातिवादान् वै तदा देवो ववर्ष ह । देवापिर्योगमास्थाय कलापग्राममाश्रितः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: vedavādātivādān vai tadā devo vavarṣa ha devāpiryogamāsthāya kalāpagrāmamāśritaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: When Devāpi spoke blasphemously about the Vedas (and their teachings), the god Indra released the showers (as Devāpi proved apostate to the Vedic religion, and ceased to have a right to rule). Devāpi (realized his mistake and adopted the path of yoga and resorted to Kalāpagrāma.

devanagari: सोमवंशे कलौ नष्टे कृतादौ स्थापयिष्यति । बाह्लीकात्सोमदत्तोऽभूद् भूरिर्भूरिश्रवास्ततः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: somavaṃśe kalau naṣṭe kṛtādau sthāpayiṣyati bāhlīkātsomadatto

translation: When Lunar race gets extinct in the Kali age, he will (re-) establish it at the advent of the next age. From Bāhlīka was born Somadatta who had three sons viz. Bhūri, Bhūriśravas and Śala. Śantanu’s son by Gaṅgā was the self-controlled Bhīṣma who was the most eminent among all the experts in dharma-śāstra, a great devotee of the Lord, a wise and learned person, a leader of a battalion of warriors by Whom even Parśurāma (his preceptor in the science of arms) was pleased with his art and skill in fighting, Śantanu begot on Satyavatī[2], the daughter of the chief of Dāśas, a son called Citrāṅgada.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
According to Mahābhārata Adi 63.50-69 she was the daughter of king Uparicara Vasu (vide V.5 above) developed in the womb of a celestial nymph Ārdrikā, cursed to be a female fish. She was brought up by the chief of fishermen and known as ‘Satyavatī’ due to her uprightness and moral virtues.

devanagari: शलश्च शन्तनोरासीद् गंगायां भीष्म आत्मवान् । सर्वधर्मविदां श्रेष्ठो महाभागवतः कविः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: śalaśca śantanorāsīd gaṃgāyāṃ bhīṣma ātmavān sarvadharmavidāṃ śreṣṭho mahābhāgavataḥ kaviḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: When Lunar race gets extinct in the Kali age, he will (re-) establish it at the advent of the next age. From Bāhlīka was born Somadatta who had three sons viz. Bhūri, Bhūriśravas and Śala. Śantanu’s son by Gaṅgā was the self-controlled Bhīṣma who was the most eminent among all the experts in dharma-śāstra, a great devotee of the Lord, a wise and learned person, a leader of a battalion of warriors by Whom even Parśurāma (his preceptor in the science of arms) was pleased with his art and skill in fighting, Śantanu begot on Satyavatī[2], the daughter of the chief of Dāśas, a son called Citrāṅgada.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
According to Mahābhārata Adi 63.50-69 she was the daughter of king Uparicara Vasu (vide V.5 above) developed in the womb of a celestial nymph Ārdrikā, cursed to be a female fish. She was brought up by the chief of fishermen and known as ‘Satyavatī’ due to her uprightness and moral virtues.

devanagari: वीरयूथाग्रणीर्येन रामोऽपि युधि तोषितः । शन्तनोर्दाशकन्यायां जज्ञे चित्रांगदः सुतः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: vīrayūthāgraṇīryena rāmo śantanordāśakanyāyāṃ jajñe citrāṃgadaḥ sutaḥ

Śantanu’s son by Gaṅgā was the self-controlled Bhīṣma who was the most eminent among all the experts in dharma-śāstra, a great devotee of the Lord, a wise and learned person, a leader of a battalion of warriors by Whom even Parśurāma (his preceptor in the science of arms) was pleased with his art and skill in fighting, Śantanu begot on Satyavatī[2], the daughter of the chief of Dāśas, a son called Citrāṅgada.

Footnote [2]:

translation: Her younger son was Vicitra-vīrya. Citrāṅgada was killed (in a duel with a Gandharva, called Citrāṅgada). Through her, from Parāśara, descended directly a digit or a ray of Lord Hari as her son (in the person of Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana).

devanagari: वेदगुप्तो मुनिः कृष्णो यतोऽहं इदमध्यगाम् । हित्वा स्वशिष्यान् पैलादीन् भगवान् बादरायणः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: vedagupto muniḥ kṛṣṇo yato hitvā svaśiṣyān pailādīn bhagavān bādarāyaṇaḥ

translation: The sage Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana is the protector of the Vedas from whom I learnt this Śrīmad Bhāgavata. In super- session of his own disciples such as Paila and others (vide Supra 1.4.21-22), the venerable sage Bādarāyaṇa preferred me, his son, as I was very serene by nature and recited (taught) to me this esoteric and sacred text (viz. the Bhāgavata Purāṇa. Then Vicitravīrya married two princesses of Kāśī, Ambā and Ambālikā both of whom were taken away by force (by Bhīṣma) from their svayaṃvara. Vicitravīrya was too much attached to them, and caught pthysis and died (in his youth).

devanagari: मह्यं पुत्राय शान्ताय परं गुह्यमिदं जगौ । विचित्रवीर्योऽथोवाह काशीराजसुते बलात् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: mahyaṃ putrāya śāntāya paraṃ guhyamidaṃ jagau vicitravīryo

translation: The sage Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana is the protector of the Vedas from whom I learnt this Śrīmad Bhāgavata. In super- session of his own disciples such as Paila and others (vide Supra 1.4.21-22), the venerable sage Bādarāyaṇa preferred me, his son, as I was very serene by nature and recited (taught) to me this esoteric and sacred text (viz. the Bhāgavata Purāṇa. Then Vicitravīrya married two princesses of Kāśī, Ambā and Ambālikā both of whom were taken away by force (by Bhīṣma) from their svayaṃvara. Vicitravīrya was too much attached to them, and caught pthysis and died (in his youth).

devanagari: स्वयंवराद् उपानीते अम्बिकाम्बालिके उभे । तयोरासक्तहृदयो गृहीतो यक्ष्मणा मृतः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: svayaṃvarād upānīte ambikāmbālike ubhe tayorāsaktahṛdayo gṛhīto yakṣmaṇā mṛtaḥ

translation: The sage Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana is the protector of the Vedas from whom I learnt this Śrīmad Bhāgavata. In super- session of his own disciples such as Paila and others (vide Supra 1.4.21-22), the venerable sage Bādarāyaṇa preferred me, his son, as I was very serene by nature and recited (taught) to me this esoteric and sacred text (viz. the Bhāgavata Purāṇa. Then Vicitravīrya married two princesses of Kāśī, Ambā and Ambālikā both of whom were taken away by force (by Bhīṣma) from their svayaṃvara. Vicitravīrya was too much attached to them, and caught pthysis and died (in his youth).

devanagari: क्षेत्रेऽप्रजस्य वै भ्रातुः मात्रोक्तो बादरायणः । धृतराष्ट्रं च पाण्डुं च विदुरं चाप्यजीजनत् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣetre dhṛtarāṣṭraṃ ca pāṇḍuṃ ca viduraṃ cāpyajījanat

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: When requested by his mother[3] (Satyavatī), Bādarāyaṇa (Vyāsa) procreated from the fields (i.e. wives) of his childless brother (Vicitravīrya)—Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Pāṇḍu and Vidura[4].
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes a religious injunction that one has to comply with such requests when instructed by elders.

Footnote [4]:
Vidura’s mother was not Vicitravīrya’s wife, but a maid servant of the widowed queen Ambikā who deputed her as her substitute to Vyāsa —Mahābhārata Ādi 1.94-96.

devanagari: गान्धार्यां धृतराष्ट्रस्य जज्ञे पुत्रशतं नृप । तत्र दुर्योधनो ज्येष्ठो दुःशला चापि कन्यका ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: gāndhāryāṃ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya jajñe putraśataṃ nṛpa tatra duryodhano jyeṣṭho duḥśalā cāpi kanyakā

translation: Dhṛtarāṣṭra had a hundred sons by his wife Gāndhārī. Of them, Duryodhana was the eldest. They had also a daughter, by name Duḥśalā.

devanagari: शापात् मैथुनरुद्धस्य पाण्डोः कुन्त्यां महारथाः । जाता धर्मानिलेन्द्रेभ्यो युधिष्ठिरमुखास्त्रयः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śāpāt maithunaruddhasya pāṇḍoḥ kuntyāṃ mahārathāḥ jātā dharmānilendrebhyo yudhiṣṭhiramukhāstrayaḥ

translation: To Pāṇḍu who was prohibited from sexual intercourse by a curse, three great (chariot-) warriors, the eldest of whom was Yudhiṣṭhira (Bhīma and Arjuna being the next two) were procreated on Kuntī from Dharma (the god Yamadharma), the wind-god and Indra.

devanagari: नकुलः सहदेवश्च माद्र्यां नासत्यदस्रयोः । द्रौपद्यां पञ्च पञ्चभ्यः पुत्रास्ते पितरोऽभवन् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: nakulaḥ sahadevaśca mādryāṃ nāsatyadasrayoḥ draupadyāṃ pañca pañcabhyaḥ putrāste pitaro
translation: Nakula and Sahadeva were born from (the loins of Aśvinī-kumāras) through Mādrī (Pāṇḍu’s second wife). Draupadī had five sons,) one from each of her five husbands)—all of them your uncles (or who were the manes in their former life).

devanagari: युधिष्ठिरात्प्रतिविन्ध्यः श्रुतसेनो वृकोदरात् । अर्जुनात् श्रुतकीर्तिस्तु शतानीकस्तु नाकुलिः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: yudhiṣṭhirātprativindhyaḥ śrutaseno vṛkodarāt arjunāt śrutakīrtistu śatānīkastu nākuliḥ
translation: (Through Draupadī) Prativindhya was born from Yudhiṣṭhira; Śrutasena, from Bhīma; Śrutakīrti from Arjuna, Śatānīka from Nakula. Śrutakarmā was the son of Sahadeva. The other sons of the Pāṇḍavas were as follows: Yudhiṣṭhira begot Devaka on Pauravī; Bhīma had Ghaṭotkaca by Hiḍimbā and Sarvagata by Kālī. Vijayā, the daughter of Parvata, gave birth to a son Suhotra from Sahadeva.

devanagari: सहदेवसुतो राजन् श्रुतकर्मा तथापरे । युधिष्ठिरात्तु पौरव्यां देवकोऽथ घटोत्कचः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: sahadevasuto rājan śrutakarmā tathāpare yudhiṣṭhirāttu pauravyāṃ devako

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: (Through Draupadī) Prativindhya was born from Yudhiṣṭhira; Śrutasena, from Bhīma; Śrutakīrti from Arjuna, Śatānīka from Nakula. Śrutakarmā was the son of Sahadeva. The other sons of the Pāṇḍavas were as follows: Yudhiṣṭhira begot Devaka on Pauravī; Bhīma had Ghaṭotkaca by Hiḍimbā and Sarvagata by Kālī. Vijayā, the daughter of Parvata, gave birth to a son Suhotra from Sahadeva.

devanagari: भीमसेनाद् हिडिम्बायां काल्यां सर्वगतस्ततः । सहदेवात् सुहोत्रं तु विजयासूत पार्वती ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: bhīmasenād hiḍimbāyāṃ kālyāṃ sarvagatastataḥ sahadevāt suhotraṃ tu vijayāsūta pārvatī

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [accusative single from √ kal class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kal ]

translation: (Through Draupadī) Prativindhya was born from Yudhiṣṭhira; Śrutasena, from Bhīma; Śrutakīrti from Arjuna, Śatānīka from Nakula. Śrutakarmā was the son of Sahadeva. The other sons of the Pāṇḍavas were as follows: Yudhiṣṭhira begot Devaka on Pauravī; Bhīma had Ghaṭotkaca by Hiḍimbā and Sarvagata by Kālī. Vijayā, the daughter of Parvata, gave birth to a son Suhotra from Sahadeva.

devanagari: करेणुमत्यां नकुलो नरमित्रं तथार्जुनः । इरावन्तमुलूप्यां वै सुतायां बभ्रुवाहनम् । मणिपुरपतेः सोऽपि तत्पुत्रः पुत्रिकासुतः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: kareṇumatyāṃ nakulo naramitraṃ tathārjunaḥ irāvantamulūpyāṃ vai sutāyāṃ babhruvāhanam maṇipurapateḥ so

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Nakula begot Nirāmitra on Kareṇumatī. So also Arjuna had Irāvān from Ulūpī and Babhruvāhana by (Citrāṅgadā) the princess of the Maṇipura king. But he was adopted as a son by his (maternal) grandfather (as that was the condition precedent of the marriage, technically called putrikāsuta)[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā, Bālaprabodhini, Bhaktamanorañjanī Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa quote the condition:
abhrātṛkām pradāsyāmi tubhyaṃ kanyām alaṅkṛtām /
asyāṃ yo jāyate putraḥ sa me putro bhaviṣyati //
‘I give in marriage my daughter duly decorated with ornaments, on condition that, as she has no brother, the son that will be born to her will be my son.

devanagari: तव तातः सुभद्रायां अभिमन्युरजायत । सर्वातिरथजिद् वीर उत्तरायां ततो भवान् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tava tātaḥ subhadrāyāṃ abhimanyurajāyata sarvātirathajid vīra uttarāyāṃ tato bhavān

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Your father Abhimanyu was born of Subhadrā. He was a hero who vanquished all ati-rathis (a chariot-warrior who defeats innumerable enemies on the battlefield). You are born of Uttarā from him.

devanagari: परिक्षीणेषु कुरुषु द्रौणेर्ब्रह्मास्त्रतेजसा । त्वं च कृष्णानुभावेन सजीवो मोचितोऽन्तकात् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: parikṣīṇeṣu kuruṣu drauṇerbrahmāstratejasā tvaṃ ca kṛṣṇānubhāvena sajīvo mocito
translation: When all the Kauravas were slain, (Aśvatthāman) the son of Droṇa discharged the missile called Brahmāstra. You were rescued alive from the god of death by the spiritual prowess of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: तवेमे तनयास्तात जनमेजयपूर्वकाः । श्रुतसेनो भीमसेन उग्रसेनश्च वीर्यवान् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: taveme tanayāstāta janamejayapūrvakāḥ śrutaseno bhīmasena ugrasenaśca vīryavān
translation: Oh child! You have the following four sons, the eldest of whom is Janamejaya, the rest being Śrutasena, Bhīmasena and Ugrasena—all of whom are valiant.

devanagari: जनमेजयस्त्वां विदित्वा तक्षकान्निधनं गतम् । सर्पान् वै सर्पयागाग्नौ स होष्यति रुषान्वितः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: janamejayastvāṃ viditvā takṣakānnidhanaṃ gatam sarpān vai sarpayāgāgnau sa hoṣyati ruṣānvitaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Knowing that you have met death from Takṣaka’s bite, he (Janamejaya) will get enraged, and will commit as oblations to fire, serpents in the serpent-sacrifice.

devanagari: कावषेयं पुरोधाय तुरं तुरगमेधयाट् । समन्तात् पृथिवीं सर्वां जित्वा यक्ष्यति चाध्वरैः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: kāvaṣeyaṃ purodhāya turaṃ turagamedhayāṭ samantāt pṛthivīṃ sarvāṃ jitvā yakṣyati cādhvaraiḥ
translation: Having conquered the earth on all sides, and appointing Tura, the son of Kavaṣa, as the sacrificial priest, Janamejaya, the performer of aśvamedha (horse-sacrifice), will worship the Lord by performance of many sacrifices.

devanagari: तस्य पुत्रः शतानीको याज्ञवल्क्यात् त्रयीं पठन् । अस्त्रज्ञानं क्रियाज्ञानं शौनकात् परमेष्यति ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya putraḥ śatānīko yājñavalkyāt trayīṃ paṭhan astrajñānaṃ kriyājñānaṃ śaunakāt parameṣyati

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ paṭh class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ paṭh class 1 verb]

translation: Janamejaya’s son Śatānīka will learn the three Vedas as well as the knowledge of religious rites from Yājñavalkya, the technique of the use of missiles (from Kṛpa), and the knowledge about the self (the spiritual science) from Śaunaka.

devanagari: सहस्रानीकस्तत्पुत्रः ततश्चैवाश्वमेधजः । असीमकृष्णस्तस्यापि नेमिचक्रस्तु तत्सुतः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: sahasrānīkastatputraḥ tataścaivāśvamedhajaḥ asīmakṛṣṇastasyāpi nemicakrastu tatsutaḥ
translation: His son will be Sahasrānīka whose son will be Aśvamedhaja; his son will be Asīma-Kṛṣṇa, whose son will be Nemicakra.

devanagari: गजाह्वये हृते नद्या कौशाम्ब्यां साधु वत्स्यति । उक्तस्ततश्चित्ररथः तस्मात् कविरथः सुतः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: gajāhvaye hṛte nadyā kauśāmbyāṃ sādhu vatsyati uktastataścitrarathaḥ tasmāt kavirathaḥ sutaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: When Hastināpura will be washed away by the floods of the river (Gaṅgā), he will settle down at Kauśāmbī (Kosam, near Allahabad). He will have Citraratha as his son, of whom a son Kaviratha will be born.

devanagari: तस्माच्च वृष्टिमांस्तस्य सुषेणोऽथ महीपतिः । सुनीथस्तस्य भविता नृचक्षुर्यत् सुखीनलः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmācca vṛṣṭimāṃstasya suṣeṇo sunīthastasya bhavitā nṛcakṣuryat sukhīnalaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: From Kaviratha will appear Vṛṣṭimān (as the son) from whom will be born king Mahīpati. Sunītha will be his son. He will have a son Nṛcakṣus whose son will be Sukhīnala.

devanagari: परिप्लवः सुतस्तस्मात् मेधावी सुनयात्मजः । नृपञ्जयस्ततो दूर्वः तिमिः तस्मात् जनिष्यति ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: pariplavaḥ sutastasmāt medhāvī sunayātmajaḥ nṛpañjayastato dūrvaḥ timiḥ tasmāt janiṣyati

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [locative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [locative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [locative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [locative single from √ jan class 4 verb]
[locative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [locative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [locative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [locative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: His son will be Pariplava, whose son will be Medhāvī who will have Sunaya as his son. Thence will be born Nṛpañjaya, from whom will be Dūrva, who will beget Timi.

devanagari: तिमेर्बृहद्रथः तस्मात् शतानीकः सुदासजः । शतानीकाद् दुर्दमनः तस्यापत्यं बहीनरः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: timerbṛhadrathaḥ tasmāt śatānīkaḥ sudāsajaḥ śatānīkād durdamanaḥ tasyāpatyaṃ bahīnaraḥ
translation: From Timi will be born Bṛhadratha whose son will be Sudāsa, the father of Śatānīka. From Śatānīka will spring Durdamana whose son will be Bahīnara.

devanagari: दण्डपाणिर्निमिस्तस्य क्षेमको भविता यतः । ब्रह्मक्षत्रस्य वै प्रोक्तो वंशो देवर्षिसत्कृतः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: daṇḍapāṇirnimistasya kṣemako bhavitā yataḥ brahmakṣatrasya vai prokto vaṃśo devarṣisatkṛtaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: His son will be Daṇḍapāṇi who will beget Nimi. From him will be born king Kṣemaka. In this way the lineage of Brāhmaṇa-cum-Kṣatriya (Lunar race from which Brāhmaṇa and Kṣatriya races started) which has been honoured and praised by gods and sages has been described to you.

devanagari: क्षेमकं प्राप्य राजानं संस्थां प्राप्स्यति वै कलौ । अथ मागधराजानो भवितारो ये वदामि ते ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣemakaṃ prāpya rājānaṃ saṃsthāṃ prāpsyati vai kalau atha māgadharājāno bhavitāro ye vadāmi te

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: When the lunar race reaches king Kṣemaka, it will be extinct in the Kali age. Now I shall describe to you the future kings of the Magadha country.

devanagari: भविता सहदेवस्य मार्जारिर्यत् श्रुतश्रवाः । ततो युतायुः तस्यापि निरमित्रोऽथ तत्सुतः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavitā sahadevasya mārjāriryat śrutaśravāḥ tato yutāyuḥ tasyāpi niramitro

translation: The son of Sahadeva (Jarāsandha’ successor—son) will be Mārjāri whose son will be Śrutaśravas. Thence will be Ayutāyu, whose son will be Niramitra.

devanagari: सुनक्षत्रः सुनक्षत्राद् बृहत्सेनोऽथ कर्मजित् । ततः सुतञ्जयाद् विप्रः शुचिस्तस्य भविष्यति ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: sunakṣatraḥ sunakṣatrād bṛhatseno tataḥ sutañjayād vipraḥ śucistasya bhaviṣyati
translation: His son will be Sunakṣatra, from whom will be born Bṛhatsena—his son will be Karmajit. Thence will be Sṛtañjaya, from whom will appear Vipra, the father of Śuci.

devanagari: क्षेमोऽथ सुव्रतस्तस्माद् धर्मसूत्रः शमस्ततः । द्युमत्सेनोऽथ सुमतिः सुबलो जनिता ततः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣemo dyumatseno

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ jan ]

translation: Then will follow Kṣema whose son will be Suvrata. His son will be born Dharmasūtra whose son will be Śama. Thence will be Dyumatsena from whom will be born Sumati, whose son will be Subala.

devanagari: सुनीथः सत्यजिदथ विश्वजित् यद् रिपुञ्जयः । बार्हद्रथाश्च भूपाला भाव्याः साहस्रवत्सरम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: sunīthaḥ satyajidatha viśvajit yad ripuñjayaḥ bārhadrathāśca bhūpālā bhāvyāḥ sāhasravatsaram

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
Thence will be Sunītha of whom Satyajit, the father of Viśvajit will be born. From Viśvajit will be born Ripuñjaya. The kings of Bṛhadratha dynasty will reign for one thousand years.
